speaker: LORENZO
text: The moon shines bright. In such a night as this,

speaker: LORENZO
text: When the sweet wind did gently kiss the trees

speaker: LORENZO
text: And they did make no noise, in such a night

speaker: LORENZO
text: Troilus, methinks, mounted the Trojan walls

speaker: LORENZO
text: And sighed his soul toward the Grecian tents

speaker: LORENZO
text: Where Cressid lay that night.

speaker: JESSICA
text: In such a night

speaker: JESSICA
text: Did Thisbe fearfully o’ertrip the dew

speaker: JESSICA
text: And saw the lion’s shadow ere himself

speaker: JESSICA
text: And ran dismayed away.

speaker: LORENZO
text: In such a night

speaker: LORENZO
text: Stood Dido with a willow in her hand

speaker: LORENZO
text: Upon the wild sea-banks, and waft her love

speaker: LORENZO
text: To come again to Carthage.

speaker: JESSICA
text: In such a night

speaker: JESSICA
text: Medea gathered the enchanted herbs

speaker: JESSICA
text: That did renew old Aeson.

speaker: LORENZO
text: In such a night

speaker: LORENZO
text: Did Jessica steal from the wealthy Jew,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And with an unthrift love did run from Venice

speaker: LORENZO
text: As far as Belmont.

speaker: JESSICA
text: In such a night

speaker: JESSICA
text: Did young Lorenzo swear he loved her well,

speaker: JESSICA
text: Stealing her soul with many vows of faith,

speaker: JESSICA
text: And ne’er a true one.

speaker: LORENZO
text: In such a night

speaker: LORENZO
text: Did pretty Jessica, like a little shrew,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Slander her love, and he forgave it her.

speaker: JESSICA
text: I would out-night you did nobody come,

speaker: JESSICA
text: But hark, I hear the footing of a man.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Who comes so fast in silence of the night?

speaker: STEPHANO
text: A friend.

speaker: LORENZO
text: A friend? What friend? Your name, I pray you, friend.

speaker: STEPHANO
text: Stephano is my name, and I bring word

speaker: STEPHANO
text: My mistress will before the break of day

speaker: STEPHANO
text: Be here at Belmont. She doth stray about

speaker: STEPHANO
text: By holy crosses, where she kneels and prays

speaker: STEPHANO
text: For happy wedlock hours.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Who comes with her?

speaker: STEPHANO
text: None but a holy hermit and her maid.

speaker: STEPHANO
text: I pray you, is my master yet returned?

speaker: LORENZO
text: He is not, nor we have not heard from him.—

speaker: LORENZO
text: But go we in, I pray thee, Jessica,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And ceremoniously let us prepare

speaker: LORENZO
text: Some welcome for the mistress of the house.

speaker: LANCELET
text: Sola, sola! Wo ha, ho! Sola, sola!

speaker: LORENZO
text: Who calls?

speaker: LANCELET
text: Sola! Did you see Master Lorenzo? Master

speaker: LANCELET
text: Lorenzo, sola, sola!

speaker: LORENZO
text: Leave holloaing, man! Here.

speaker: LANCELET
text: Sola! Where, where?

speaker: LORENZO
text: Here!

speaker: LANCELET
text: Tell him there’s a post come from my master

speaker: LANCELET
text: with his horn full of good news. My master will

speaker: LANCELET
text: be here ere morning, sweet soul.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Let’s in, and there expect their coming.

speaker: LORENZO
text: And yet no matter; why should we go in?—

speaker: LORENZO
text: My friend Stephano, signify, I pray you,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Within the house, your mistress is at hand,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And bring your music forth into the air.

speaker: LORENZO
text: How sweet the moonlight sleeps upon this bank.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Here will we sit and let the sounds of music

speaker: LORENZO
text: Creep in our ears; soft stillness and the night

speaker: LORENZO
text: Become the touches of sweet harmony.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Sit, Jessica. Look how the floor of heaven

speaker: LORENZO
text: Is thick inlaid with patens of bright gold.

speaker: LORENZO
text: There’s not the smallest orb which thou behold’st

speaker: LORENZO
text: But in his motion like an angel sings,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Still choiring to the young-eyed cherubins.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Such harmony is in immortal souls,

speaker: LORENZO
text: But whilst this muddy vesture of decay

speaker: LORENZO
text: Doth grossly close it in, we cannot hear it.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Come, ho! and wake Diana with a hymn.

speaker: LORENZO
text: With sweetest touches pierce your mistress’ ear,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And draw her home with music.

speaker: JESSICA
text: I am never merry when I hear sweet music.

speaker: LORENZO
text: The reason is, your spirits are attentive.

speaker: LORENZO
text: For do but note a wild and wanton herd

speaker: LORENZO
text: Or race of youthful and unhandled colts,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Fetching mad bounds, bellowing and neighing loud,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Which is the hot condition of their blood,

speaker: LORENZO
text: If they but hear perchance a trumpet sound,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Or any air of music touch their ears,

speaker: LORENZO
text: You shall perceive them make a mutual stand,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Their savage eyes turned to a modest gaze

speaker: LORENZO
text: By the sweet power of music. Therefore the poet

speaker: LORENZO
text: Did feign that Orpheus drew trees, stones, and floods,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Since naught so stockish, hard, and full of rage,

speaker: LORENZO
text: But music for the time doth change his nature.

speaker: LORENZO
text: The man that hath no music in himself,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Nor is not moved with concord of sweet sounds,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Is fit for treasons, stratagems, and spoils;

speaker: LORENZO
text: The motions of his spirit are dull as night,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And his affections dark as Erebus.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Let no such man be trusted. Mark the music.

speaker: PORTIA
text: That light we see is burning in my hall.

speaker: PORTIA
text: How far that little candle throws his beams!

speaker: PORTIA
text: So shines a good deed in a naughty world.

speaker: NERISSA
text: When the moon shone we did not see the candle.

speaker: PORTIA
text: So doth the greater glory dim the less.

speaker: PORTIA
text: A substitute shines brightly as a king

speaker: PORTIA
text: Until a king be by, and then his state

speaker: PORTIA
text: Empties itself as doth an inland brook

speaker: PORTIA
text: Into the main of waters. Music, hark!

speaker: NERISSA
text: It is your music, madam, of the house.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nothing is good, I see, without respect.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Methinks it sounds much sweeter than by day.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Silence bestows that virtue on it, madam.

speaker: PORTIA
text: The crow doth sing as sweetly as the lark

speaker: PORTIA
text: When neither is attended, and I think

speaker: PORTIA
text: The nightingale, if she should sing by day

speaker: PORTIA
text: When every goose is cackling, would be thought

speaker: PORTIA
text: No better a musician than the wren.

speaker: PORTIA
text: How many things by season seasoned are

speaker: PORTIA
text: To their right praise and true perfection!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Peace—how the moon sleeps with Endymion

speaker: PORTIA
text: And would not be awaked!

speaker: LORENZO
text: That is the voice,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Or I am much deceived, of Portia.

speaker: PORTIA
text: He knows me as the blind man knows the cuckoo,

speaker: PORTIA
text: By the bad voice.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Dear lady, welcome home.

speaker: PORTIA
text: We have been praying for our husbands’ welfare,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which speed we hope the better for our words.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Are they returned?

speaker: LORENZO
text: Madam, they are not yet,

speaker: LORENZO
text: But there is come a messenger before

speaker: LORENZO
text: To signify their coming.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Go in, Nerissa.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Give order to my servants that they take

speaker: PORTIA
text: No note at all of our being absent hence—

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nor you, Lorenzo—Jessica, nor you.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Your husband is at hand. I hear his trumpet.

speaker: LORENZO
text: We are no tell-tales, madam, fear you not.

speaker: PORTIA
text: This night methinks is but the daylight sick;

speaker: PORTIA
text: It looks a little paler. ’Tis a day

speaker: PORTIA
text: Such as the day is when the sun is hid.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: We should hold day with the Antipodes

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If you would walk in absence of the sun.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Let me give light, but let me not be light,

speaker: PORTIA
text: For a light wife doth make a heavy husband,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And never be Bassanio so for me.

speaker: PORTIA
text: But God sort all! You are welcome home, my lord.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I thank you, madam. Give welcome to my friend.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: This is the man, this is Antonio,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: To whom I am so infinitely bound.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You should in all sense be much bound to him,

speaker: PORTIA
text: For as I hear he was much bound for you.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: No more than I am well acquitted of.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Sir, you are very welcome to our house.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It must appear in other ways than words;

speaker: PORTIA
text: Therefore I scant this breathing courtesy.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: By yonder moon I swear you do me wrong!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: In faith, I gave it to the judge’s clerk.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Would he were gelt that had it, for my part,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Since you do take it, love, so much at heart.

speaker: PORTIA
text: A quarrel ho, already! What’s the matter?

speaker: GRATIANO
text: About a hoop of gold, a paltry ring

speaker: GRATIANO
text: That she did give me, whose posy was

speaker: GRATIANO
text: For all the world like cutler’s poetry

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Upon a knife, “Love me, and leave me not.”

speaker: NERISSA
text: What talk you of the posy or the value?

speaker: NERISSA
text: You swore to me when I did give it you

speaker: NERISSA
text: That you would wear it till your hour of death,

speaker: NERISSA
text: And that it should lie with you in your grave.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Though not for me, yet for your vehement oaths,

speaker: NERISSA
text: You should have been respective and have kept it.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Gave it a judge’s clerk! No, God’s my judge,

speaker: NERISSA
text: The clerk will ne’er wear hair on ’s face that had it.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: He will, an if he live to be a man.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Ay, if a woman live to be a man.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Now, by this hand, I gave it to a youth,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: A kind of boy, a little scrubbèd boy,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: No higher than thyself, the judge’s clerk,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: A prating boy that begged it as a fee.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: I could not for my heart deny it him.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You were to blame, I must be plain with you,

speaker: PORTIA
text: To part so slightly with your wife’s first gift,

speaker: PORTIA
text: A thing stuck on with oaths upon your finger,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And so riveted with faith unto your flesh.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I gave my love a ring and made him swear

speaker: PORTIA
text: Never to part with it, and here he stands.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I dare be sworn for him he would not leave it

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nor pluck it from his finger for the wealth

speaker: PORTIA
text: That the world masters. Now, in faith, Gratiano,

speaker: PORTIA
text: You give your wife too unkind a cause of grief.

speaker: PORTIA
text: An ’twere to me I should be mad at it.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Why, I were best to cut my left hand off

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And swear I lost the ring defending it.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: My Lord Bassanio gave his ring away

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Unto the judge that begged it, and indeed

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Deserved it, too. And then the boy, his clerk,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: That took some pains in writing, he begged mine,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: And neither man nor master would take aught

speaker: GRATIANO
text: But the two rings.

speaker: PORTIA
text: What ring gave you, my lord?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Not that, I hope, which you received of me.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If I could add a lie unto a fault,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I would deny it, but you see my finger

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Hath not the ring upon it. It is gone.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Even so void is your false heart of truth.

speaker: PORTIA
text: By heaven, I will ne’er come in your bed

speaker: PORTIA
text: Until I see the ring!

speaker: NERISSA
text: Nor I in yours

speaker: NERISSA
text: Till I again see mine!

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Sweet Portia,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If you did know to whom I gave the ring,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If you did know for whom I gave the ring,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And would conceive for what I gave the ring,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And how unwillingly I left the ring,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: When naught would be accepted but the ring,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: You would abate the strength of your displeasure.

speaker: PORTIA
text: If you had known the virtue of the ring,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Or half her worthiness that gave the ring,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Or your own honor to contain the ring,

speaker: PORTIA
text: You would not then have parted with the ring.

speaker: PORTIA
text: What man is there so much unreasonable,

speaker: PORTIA
text: If you had pleased to have defended it

speaker: PORTIA
text: With any terms of zeal, wanted the modesty

speaker: PORTIA
text: To urge the thing held as a ceremony?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nerissa teaches me what to believe:

speaker: PORTIA
text: I’ll die for ’t but some woman had the ring!

speaker: BASSANIO
text: No, by my honor, madam, by my soul,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: No woman had it, but a civil doctor,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Which did refuse three thousand ducats of me

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And begged the ring, the which I did deny him

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And suffered him to go displeased away,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Even he that had held up the very life

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Of my dear friend. What should I say, sweet lady?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I was enforced to send it after him.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I was beset with shame and courtesy.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: My honor would not let ingratitude

speaker: BASSANIO
text: So much besmear it. Pardon me, good lady,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: For by these blessèd candles of the night,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Had you been there, I think you would have begged

speaker: BASSANIO
text: The ring of me to give the worthy doctor.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Let not that doctor e’er come near my house!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Since he hath got the jewel that I loved,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And that which you did swear to keep for me,

speaker: PORTIA
text: I will become as liberal as you:

speaker: PORTIA
text: I’ll not deny him anything I have,

speaker: PORTIA
text: No, not my body, nor my husband’s bed.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Know him I shall, I am well sure of it.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Lie not a night from home. Watch me like Argus.

speaker: PORTIA
text: If you do not, if I be left alone,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Now by mine honor, which is yet mine own,

speaker: PORTIA
text: I’ll have that doctor for my bedfellow.

speaker: NERISSA
text: And I his clerk. Therefore be well advised

speaker: NERISSA
text: How you do leave me to mine own protection.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Well, do you so. Let not me take him, then,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: For if I do, I’ll mar the young clerk’s pen.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I am th’ unhappy subject of these quarrels.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Sir, grieve not you. You are welcome notwithstanding.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Portia, forgive me this enforcèd wrong,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And in the hearing of these many friends

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I swear to thee, even by thine own fair eyes,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Wherein I see myself—

speaker: PORTIA
text: Mark you but that!

speaker: PORTIA
text: In both my eyes he doubly sees himself,

speaker: PORTIA
text: In each eye one. Swear by your double self,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And there’s an oath of credit.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Nay, but hear me.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Pardon this fault, and by my soul I swear

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I never more will break an oath with thee.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I once did lend my body for his wealth,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Which but for him that had your husband’s ring

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Had quite miscarried. I dare be bound again,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: My soul upon the forfeit, that your lord

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Will never more break faith advisedly.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Then you shall be his surety. Give him this,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And bid him keep it better than the other.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Here, Lord Bassanio, swear to keep this ring.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: By heaven, it is the same I gave the doctor!

speaker: PORTIA
text: I had it of him. Pardon me, Bassanio,

speaker: PORTIA
text: For by this ring, the doctor lay with me.

speaker: NERISSA
text: And pardon me, my gentle Gratiano,

speaker: NERISSA
text: For that same scrubbèd boy, the doctor’s clerk,

speaker: NERISSA
text: In lieu of this, last night did lie with me.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Why, this is like the mending of highways

speaker: GRATIANO
text: In summer, where the ways are fair enough!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: What, are we cuckolds ere we have deserved it?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Speak not so grossly.—You are all amazed.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Here is a letter; read it at your leisure.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It comes from Padua from Bellario.

speaker: PORTIA
text: There you shall find that Portia was the doctor,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nerissa there, her clerk. Lorenzo here

speaker: PORTIA
text: Shall witness I set forth as soon as you,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And even but now returned. I have not yet

speaker: PORTIA
text: Entered my house.—Antonio, you are welcome,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And I have better news in store for you

speaker: PORTIA
text: Than you expect. Unseal this letter soon.

speaker: PORTIA
text: There you shall find three of your argosies

speaker: PORTIA
text: Are richly come to harbor suddenly.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You shall not know by what strange accident

speaker: PORTIA
text: I chancèd on this letter.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I am dumb.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Were you the doctor and I knew you not?

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Were you the clerk that is to make me cuckold?

speaker: NERISSA
text: Ay, but the clerk that never means to do it,

speaker: NERISSA
text: Unless he live until he be a man.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Sweet doctor, you shall be my bedfellow.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: When I am absent, then lie with my wife.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Sweet lady, you have given me life and living;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: For here I read for certain that my ships

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Are safely come to road.

speaker: PORTIA
text: How now, Lorenzo?

speaker: PORTIA
text: My clerk hath some good comforts too for you.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Ay, and I’ll give them him without a fee.

speaker: NERISSA
text: There do I give to you and Jessica,

speaker: NERISSA
text: From the rich Jew, a special deed of gift,

speaker: NERISSA
text: After his death, of all he dies possessed of.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Fair ladies, you drop manna in the way

speaker: LORENZO
text: Of starvèd people.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is almost morning,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And yet I am sure you are not satisfied

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of these events at full. Let us go in,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And charge us there upon inter’gatories,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And we will answer all things faithfully.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Let it be so. The first inter’gatory

speaker: GRATIANO
text: That my Nerissa shall be sworn on is

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Whether till the next night she had rather stay

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Or go to bed now, being two hours to day.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: But were the day come, I should wish it dark

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Till I were couching with the doctor’s clerk.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Well, while I live, I’ll fear no other thing

speaker: GRATIANO
text: So sore as keeping safe Nerissa’s ring.
